    prefix = [0] * (n + 1)

    # - Let L,R be the min/max differing positions

    #   Actually [L,R] has some ones count ones_LR. We need ones_LR <= k and 
    #   we can extend to get exactly k ones. Since extending adds more ones from s,
    #   we need ones_LR <= k <= total_ones... but also t[L..R] must have k - (ones outside [L,R] but inside window) ones...

    #

    # The set of strings it produces = {strings that equal s outside [l,r] and have k ones inside [l,r]}
    # = {strings that equal s outside [l,r] and have any arrangement of k ones, z zeros inside}

    # Any string from inner window: equals s outside [l2,r2], has k ones inside [l2,r2].
    # Is it achievable by outer window? The outer window string must equal s outside [l1,r1].
    # The inner string equals s in [l1,l2) and (r2,r1] (since it equals s outside [l2,r2]).
    # So yes, the inner string is achievable by outer window too (just keep s's chars in [l1,l2) and (r2,r1]).
